item.title: Balancing fisherman tutorial
item.description:
Made a few of these for fishing friends. You could adapt to other designs.
4d 1-1/4” finish nails. 8d 2-1/2” framing nail. 1/4” round stock, 1/8x1/2 flat bar - 1” long. Not pictured, 11” length of wire and 1-1/2” long piece of flat bar to make the fish.
Cut two of the finish nails to 1” length. Cut the framing nail head and point off.
Bend the 1” cut nails as shown. Layout against the flat bar. I put them both in a vise and hammer them over at the same time so they’re consistent.
Weld the “hands” together.
Put a small bend in the elbows.
Set the arms at the angle you like and weld to body.
Set the legs and weld into place.
For the head, round it as desired before cutting. Easier to hold that way.
Cut it off to whatever length you like.
I welded a bead on the bottom of the head to make a neck. You don’t have to.
Maybe shoulda welded the head before the arms. I welded from the back.
Weld the fishing rod as shown. Don’t worry too much about the angle. Everything can be bent to adjust later.
Cut a piece of wire about 11” long. Bend a curve in it around a 4” radius.
Weld the wire to the rod at about a right angle.
I cut the fish out of the same flat bar, 1-1/2” long. After cutting I polished it up with a flapper wheel. I used a sanding disc on the dremel to give the sorta fish scale design.
Lay it flat and weld the fish to the wire.
I heated it with a torch to get a nice blue/purple oxide. While it’s hot, I apply some oil to help prevent rust.
More blue oxide.
item.dc:creator: bloomautomatic

item.title: this is my dog on drugs
item.description: Our old man usually gets a little trazodone on fireworks holidays, to help him deal. He's kind of a lightweight and 50mg will zonk him out. He's also started to experience some sundowning recently - probably the start of some age-related cognitive dysfunction. So he might start getting a little evening treat to take some of the edge off.
